creator: gennady.marchenko@itv.ru
text:
>Please, provide more information why do you think this port is broken, 
>preferably with the reproducable scenarios. So far I see no reasons for 
>such serious claims.

OK. We have 2 samba server PDC & BDC version 3.0.24 with openldap
2.3.31/2.3.35 with background sync between. Everything works fine.
One day I have noticed about new version of Samba in ports.-> I have
installed Samba port v. 3.0.25a on my BDC, after succesfull setup proceess
and restart samba service I check BDC membership in domain by wbinfo -t -
result was:

checking the trust secret via RPC calls failed error code was
NT_STATUS_INVALID_HANDLE (0xc0000008) Could not check secret

And log was:  _net_auth2: creds_server_check failed. Rejecting auth request
from client BDC machine account BDC$


I think "OK, now I add BDC server to my DOMAIN and everything will be ok",
and try to add samba 3.0.25a in domain controlled by Samba 3.0.24 with
command:
net join DOMAIN -U root

And got:

utils/net_rpc_join.c:net_rpc_join_newstyle(304)
  error setting trust account password: NT code 0x1c010002 Unable to join
domain DOMAIN

And log was: _net_auth2: failed to get machine password for account BDC$:
NT_STATUS_ACCESS_DENIED

I think "OK, now I delete machine account and recreate it by adding BDC to
domain again"

And got it problem again. I went to the Internet and try to simple search in
Google and result wasn't very good:

Many people have some problem with 3.0.25a and 3.0.25b with auth in PDC
(older version), and with auth WINXP client.

In this day my team-mate has some problem with adding Samba 3.0.25a to our
DOMAIN in another town (our branch office) - this operation some weeks ago
was successfully more than 20 times with any other versions of samba.

Now we are working without our BDC server (cause I can't update PDC to
strange 3.0.25a - many people will be very angry if it fail.) and downgrade
to samba version 3.0.24 in our new branch office (it was fasted trouble
resolve). I wait for your help. ;)


P.S sorry for my English please.

Best Wishes,
Gennady.
